parameters: BEN_Base.inference accepts refine_foreground and defaults it to false.
The README says refinement costs time but can improve matting edges.
inference accepts a PIL image or an image list.
The README recommends no more than three images for consumer GPUs.
Default postprocess uses postprocess_image; refine mode uses raw sigmoid alpha plus refine_foreground_process.
Official preprocessing converts to RGB, resizes to 1024x1024 with LANCZOS, normalizes with ImageNet mean/std, and uses float16 on CUDA or float32 on CPU.
refine_foreground_process exposes initial blur radius r=90.
postprocess_image bilinearly restores alpha and applies per-image min-max normalization.
Pinned Base safetensors weight and model configuration.

dataset.metric_note: Quality values are controlled-green-screen pseudo metrics; boundary quality also received visual review.

parameters.alpha_postprocess: bilinear restore plus per-image min-max normalization

notes: This retains BEN2's official refine_foreground=false default.
The accepted CUDA command held the GPU benchmark lock after two acquisition retries.
Fastest end-to-end option, but it retains much more green in soft-edge foreground RGB.

parameters.alpha_postprocess: raw sigmoid to 8-bit PIL alpha, then nearest resize

notes: Runtime and VRAM are from the required independent GPU-lock recheck; the first locked matrix run measured 259.699 ms inference and 711.169 ms end to end.
Refinement reduces green fringe by about 87 percent versus the official no-refine default, but its CPU foreground estimator roughly doubles total latency.
The 24-frame temporal run held the GPU lock and used the same parameters.

parameters.alpha_postprocess: raw sigmoid to 8-bit PIL alpha, then nearest resize

notes: Held the shared GPU benchmark lock.
Alpha is identical to r=90 because radius only changes foreground RGB estimation.
Slightly faster and still much cleaner than no-refine, but green fringe is higher than at the official r=90 default.

recommendation.reason: This is the highest-quality tested BEN2 output: it slightly improves alpha pseudo metrics and cuts green fringe from 0.036927 to 0.004709. The official r=90 radius is cleaner than r=45. Use the no-refine default instead only when the roughly 412 ms/frame recheck latency cost is unacceptable or foreground RGB is refined elsewhere.
recommendation.scope_note: The official Base implementation fixes network input at 1024x1024; no unsupported alternate model resolution was introduced.

failed_attempts.error: Two lock-acquisition attempts returned OSError [Errno 36] before model launch
Initial lock-acquisition attempts returned OSError [Errno 36] before model launch
